content:
# Agentic Retrieval Protocol -- Design Decisions (2026-03-31)

## What: Server-side intelligence for agent-corvia interactions
Push complex retrieval logic server-side (deterministic, portable). Keep agent instructions minimal (~20 lines). Five design areas across two tracks.

## Key Decisions:
1. Quality signals (confidence/suggestion/gap_detected) computed server-side in corvia_search response. Thresholds: high >= 0.65+3 results, medium >= 0.45, low = else.
2. Suggestion generation is template-based, not LLM-generated (zero latency cost).
3. corvia_context gets max_tokens param (hard cap 4000) + compact format for subagent prompts.
4. Gap detection is implicit from search logs (ring buffer), surfaced in dashboard. NOT knowledge entries.
5. Write deduplication at cosine > 0.90 blocks write; 0.80-0.90 warns; force_write bypasses.
6. No explicit rating protocol for feedback. Implicit signals only (re-search patterns, write correlation).

## Alternatives rejected:
- LLM-based quality assessment (unreliable at ~70%)
- Explicit gap reporting by agents (<20% compliance)
- Time-based write triggers (no reliable timer in agent frameworks)
- Write reminders (MCP is request-response, server cannot push)
